understanding.goal_understanding: Progress through KA59’s levels by maneuvering the controllable pieces so that a pushable block/object ends up on the goal tile(s) marked with '+', triggering the engine’s WIN condition within the step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each turn controls the currently selected piece (shown as 'P' in the semantic grid) and movement attempts shift it one grid cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction. If a pushable block is directly adjacent in the move direction and the space beyond it is free, the block is pushed along with the selected piece; otherwise movement is blocked. Walls/boundaries ('#') generally prevent movement, though some wall-like edges may have special behavior. Levels can include multiple selectable pieces ('S'); using CLICK on a selectable’s pixel position switches control to it. The movement_blocked flags act as hints about adjacency but don’t guarantee the move will fail or succeed.
